data.title: M&A News: Baker Hughes (BKR) Stock Rises on $1.45B Waygate Sale to Hexagon
data.description:
Shares of Baker Hughes (BKR) are rising in pre-market trading on Monday after the company announced a major asset sale. The company has agreed to sell its Waygate Technologies unit to Sweden-based Hexagon (HXGBY) for about $1.45 billion in cash. Through this and other recent deals, Baker Hughes is streamlining its business to boost cash flow, strengthen its balance sheet, and support long-term growth. As of this writing, BKR stock is up about 0.43% in pre-market trading.

For context, Baker Hughes is an energy technology company that provides equipment and services to oil, gas, and industrial sectors worldwide. Meanwhile, Hexagon is a global industrial technology firm that provides digital solutions for measurement, imaging, and data analytics across multiple industries.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Hims & Hers (HIMS) Shifts Beyond GLP-1 Controversy to Long-Term Health Platform
data.description:
Hims & Hers (HIMS) is moving from the controversy surrounding glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) to a long-term health platform. Not that long ago, it looked like a vulnerable growth name with a legal cloud over it. Now the picture is different. The business looks more credible, more connected, and better positioned for the long run.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

We have moved from debating how Hims & Hers fits around compounded weight-loss drugs to whether it can turn its digital reach into a durable position across branded treatments and the emerging demand for longevity. That is why, despite its near-term market volatility, I remain bullish on HIMS stock.
data.author: Nikolaos Sismanis

data.title: CarMax Is About to Report Q4 Earnings. Options Trades Expect a Major Move in KMX Stock
data.description:
Used-car retailer CarMax (KMX) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of Fiscal 2026 before the market opens on Tuesday, April 14. KMX stock has risen 21% year-to-date, driven by optimism about the company's turnaround efforts and improving demand amid geopolitical tensions and macro uncertainty. Last week, CarMax announced the appointment of two independent directors and reached a settlement with activist investor Starboard.

data.title: McDonald's (MCD) Stock Looks Stuck — Can Its Energy Drink Push Finally Move It?
data.description:
McDonald's (MCD) may be making a small change that could have a bigger impact on its U.S. business. According to the Wall Street Journal, the fast-food giant is adding energy drinks and specialty sodas to its menu, including options like a Red Bull-based beverage, as it looks to expand into higher-margin beverage sales. MCD stock hasn't done much lately, especially as investors have focused on AI and other fast-growing sectors. But this move into drinks could help drive more customer traffic and improve margins over time, something long-term investors may not be fully pricing in yet.

For context, McDonald's is a global fast-food company known for its burgers, fries, and growing focus on high-margin beverages and franchise-driven profits. Year-to-date, MCD stock is basically flat, up just 0.02%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Delta Air Lines (DAL) Justifies a Premium as Pricing Offsets Cost Pressure
data.description:
Delta Air Lines (DAL) continues to justify a premium valuation, as its pricing power proves strong enough to offset rising cost pressures. The stock jumped by double digits after reporting its Q1 results — topping both earnings per share (EPS) and revenue estimates — despite strong pressure from higher fuel costs.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

What the market saw, above all, was a demonstration of Delta's unusual combination of pricing power, disciplined capacity management, and resilient demand. This allows the company to absorb part of the cost pressure without materially compromising its earnings profile. The company's pricing resilience does not appear to be a one-quarter story. In a sector historically defined by volatility and weak pricing, DAL increasingly stands out as a structural outlier, supporting a sustained premium and my bullish view.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: Is TSMC (TSM) Stock a Buy Ahead of Q1 Earnings — or Too Late?
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., or TSMC (TSM), is set to report its first-quarter 2026 earnings on Thursday, April 16. While the company has already reported strong revenue for the quarter, investors will now focus closely on margins and profitability. According to its March report, TSMC's Q1 revenue jumped about 35% year over year, coming in at T$1.134 trillion ($35.71 billion), beating market expectations. Looking ahead, TSM's bullish outlook is backed by continued demand for advanced semiconductors used in AI data centers, high-performance computing, and next-generation smartphones. Analysts expect an average 14% upside, while the most bullish estimates suggest the stock could rise by roughly 27%.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

Trade NVDA with leverage

For context, TSMC is the world's largest contract chipmaker. The company supplies advanced semiconductors to top tech giants, including Apple (AAPL) and Nvidia (NVDA). Over the past 12 months, TSM stock has surged more than 135%.

What to Expect from TSM's Q1 Earnings

Wall Street expects Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing to report earnings of $3.29 per share, up more than 50% from a year ago.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why BofA Sees Strong Q1 Earnings for U.S. Banks
data.description:
Major U.S. banks are heading into the first-quarter earnings season with strong momentum. Analysts at Bank of America (BofA) expect the sector to deliver solid results, supported by healthy trading activity, steady investment banking, and stable credit quality.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

At the same time, deal activity has remained strong, with several large M&A transactions boosting investment banking fees.

BofA highlights three key factors driving earnings growth:

  • Rates: Higher interest rates allow banks to charge more on loans while deposit costs rise more slowly, helping boost net interest income.
  • Regulations: Clearer rules are giving banks better clarity on how much capital they need to hold.
  • Rebounding activity: Consumer and business demand remains steady, with people still spending and companies continuing to borrow and invest despite market volatility.

While these trends are positive, the firm notes that visibility remains somewhat limited due to macro uncertainty. Rising geopolitical tensions and higher oil prices could weigh on the outlook in the coming quarters. If growth slows or inflation stays high, it could hurt lending activity and increase loan losses. In that case, bank stocks could come under pressure.

BofA's Top Bank Picks

According to Bank of America, not all banks are equally positioned heading into earnings, with some expected to perform better than others:

Citigroup (C): Citi is BofA's top pick, supported by restructuring efforts and company-specific catalysts. Investors are closely watching how management executes its turnaround plans. The bank is set to report Q1 results on April 14, with Wall Street expecting EPS of $2.63, up 34% year-over-year, on strong revenue growth.

Morgan Stanley (MS): Morgan Stanley is preferred over Goldman Sachs (GS), driven by strength in its wealth management business and potential upside from IPO activity. Wall Street expects the bank to report EPS of $3.02 on revenue of $19.74 billion.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Wells Fargo (WFC): Both banks are expected to deliver in-line or better results, in line with the broader sector trend. They are scheduled to report on April 14. Analysts expect JPMorgan to post EPS of $5.45 on revenue of $49.13 billion, while Wells Fargo is projected to report EPS of $1.58 on revenue of $21.79 billion.

Wall Street Outlook for Bank Stocks Ahead of Q1 Earnings

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: JPMorgan Is About to Report Q1 Earnings Tomorrow. Options Traders Are Expecting a 3.87% Move in JPM Stock
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM), the largest U.S. bank based on assets, is scheduled to announce its results for the first quarter of 2026 on April 14, 2026. The stock is down 3% in the year-to-date as investors fret about the cost of its AI investment plans and geopolitical uncertainty from the conflict in Iran. According to TipRanks' Options Tool, options traders expect about a 3.87% move in either direction for JPM stock in reaction to Q1 2026 results.

data.title: Why Are Stock Market Futures Tumbling Today, 4/12/26?
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Sunday night after Donald Trump announced a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, following failed weekend peace talks between the U.S. and Iran that raised concerns about a longer conflict. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.99%, 0.91%, and 0.88%, respectively, at 9:00 p.m. EDT on April 12.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

In a post on Truth Social, Trump said the U.S. Navy would begin blocking ships entering or leaving the Strait of Hormuz. He added that other countries would join the effort and accused Iran of using the route for "illegal extortion."

The collapse of talks in Islamabad has increased fears that tensions between the U.S. and Iran could drag on. This has already pushed oil prices sharply higher, adding pressure on the global economy. The international oil benchmark Brent (CM:BZ) rose 8.01% to $102.83 per barrel, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) crude (CM:CL) gained 8.3% to $104.57 per barrel.

Wall Street had just wrapped up a strong week, as optimism around a temporary ceasefire lifted markets and helped all three major indexes post solid gains, with the S&P 500 up 3.6%, the Nasdaq rising about 4.7%, and the Dow gaining 3%.

Now, investor focus is also turning to earnings. The first-quarter reporting season begins this week, led by major banks. Goldman Sachs (GS) is set to report on Monday, followed by Citigroup (C), Wells Fargo (WFC), JPMorgan Chase (JPM), Morgan Stanley (MS), and Bank of America (BAC) later in the week.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, April 12
data.description:
Last week was marked by rising indexes; however, it ended on a mixed note, with stocks holding firm while oil and inflation remained in focus. The Dow Jones (DJIA) fell 0.56% to 47,916, while the S&P 500 (SPX) slipped 0.11% and the Nasdaq (NDX) rose 0.14%. At the same time, the U.S. 10-year yield moved up to 4.32%, and oil prices (CM:CL) held near $95.

This setup reflects a market that is steady on the surface but still shaped by energy risk and rate pressure.

Meanwhile, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) traded near $73,000, and gold (CM:XAUUSD) held close to record levels, suggesting that some investors are still seeking hedges.
data.author: Ran Melamed
